utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: hello mrs. lee , i see you're here for back pain .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yes .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: hey dragon , i'm seeing mrs. lee today for , uh , back pain . she's a 40-year-old female . so can you tell me what happened to you ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yes . i was at a birthday party and it was surprise party . so we were all like crunched behind and hiding . and when the person came in we all jumped out , and then i fell down the little stair and i rammed my back into the railing of the s- , uh , staircase .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: so it was a surprise for you too ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yes it was .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: very good . okay . so , um , did it hurt because you hit it , or did you twist ? what happened when you ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: i think it was my motion-

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: . of jumping up , or something .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: and maybe a twisting .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . any pain shooting down your legs at all ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: no .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: is it on one side or the other of your back ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: um , it's more on the right .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: on the right . is there anything that makes it better or worse ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: uh , if i'm lying flat it hurts a little more , and any twisting motion for sure .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . all right . and do you have any history or any back problems before ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: i did . i had a fusion done .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay , yeah that lumbar fusion . um , let's take an x-ray . hey . or let's look at an x-ray . hey dragon , show me the latest x-ray . okay , we can see where you had your fusion , t4 here on the x-ray . but i do n't see anything that looks , um , significantly abnormal . it does n't appear to have any compression fractures or anything like that . so , it's essentially , except for the fusion , a normal exam . let me do your exam for you here . can i have you stand up please ? does it hurt when i twist you that way ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yes .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . you can sit back down . if i lift your leg here , does that bother you ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: no .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: how 'bout when i lift this leg ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: no .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: any pain shooting down your legs when i do that ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: no .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . and can you feel me touching down here .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yes .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay , great . so , you had a injury to your back when you were twisting at the birthday party . you do n't really have any pain or numbness down your legs . you have some stiffness with rotation , um , but you do n't have any evidence of a , um , what we would call a pinched nerved or radiculopathy . so , i think you basically have a strain of your lower back . um , have you been taking any medications ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: just some ibuprofen .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . and do you have any other , um , medical problems ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: um i take medication for anxiety .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . and that's all ? okay . um , so what we're gon na do is i'm gon na give you , um , just continue the motrin at 800mg , three times a day , with food . i want you to use some ice and heat , alternating on your back . but i do want you walking .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: um , and , uh , this should sort of take care of itself after a few days . if it's not , or it's getting worse , i want you to come back and see me over the next week .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: does that sound good ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: it does .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay , dragon . hey dragon , go ahead and order the medications and procedures and close out the note . come on i'll bring you out to check out .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: thank you .

detail_list.summary: Back pain
The patient is a 40-year-old female presenting with back pain.
The doctor asks the patient to describe the mechanism of injury.
The patient fell and rammed her back into a staircase railing while twisting.
The doctor asks if the pain was caused by impact or twisting.
The patient reports the injury involved a twisting motion.
The doctor asks if the patient has pain radiating down her legs.
The patient denies pain radiating down her legs.
The doctor asks if the pain is localized to one side of the back.
The patient reports the pain is primarily on the right side.
The doctor asks about aggravating or alleviating factors for the pain.
The patient reports that lying flat and twisting motions aggravate the pain.
The doctor asks about prior back problems.
The patient has a history of lumbar fusion.
Lumbar spine X-rays show no acute fracture and hardware from a prior fusion in good position.
The doctor asks if twisting causes pain during the physical exam.
The doctor asks if lifting the leg causes pain.
The straight leg raise test is negative.
The doctor asks if there is pain shooting down the legs during the exam.
The doctor checks for sensation in the lower extremities.
Sensation is intact.
The doctor assesses the patient with a lumbar spine strain.
The doctor asks if the patient is taking any medications.
The patient is taking ibuprofen.
The doctor asks about other medical problems.
The patient has a history of anxiety.
The patient is instructed to take Motrin 800 mg three times a day with food.
The doctor recommends alternating ice and heat and walking.
The patient should follow up in one week if symptoms persist or worsen.
